investigation_summary: Analysis of duplicate OFC #6 entries with conflicting text

details.text_preview: (1) Determine the facility's susceptibility to blast. Consult Reference Manual to Mitigate Potential Terrorist Attacks Against Buildings (FEMA 426/BIPS 06)...
details.context_question: Can any vehicle be placed (legally or illegally) within 400 feet of the facility? / Yes / Is parking: Uncontrolled / Placement Type: Adjacent on street

details.text_preview: Evaluate the need to implement enhanced protective measures for water system components, such as intrusion detection sensors and CCTV.
details.context_question: Is external water required for the Facility Core Operations (Produce Key Services, Goods)? / Yes / Are components of the water supply located outside of the building (but still within control of facility) are protected from vandalism or accidental damage? / No

analysis.text_similarity: Completely different topics - blast analysis vs water system protection
possible_causes: Source HTML may have reused OFC ref #6 for different OFC texts in different contexts
Parser extracted different text from different HTML table cells
Data quality issue: same reference number used for semantically different OFCs
analysis.validation_behavior: Correctly flagged as ERROR per requirements: 'if duplicates have different text/URLs -> ERROR'
analysis.recommendation: Review source HTML to determine if this is expected (same ref_num for different contexts) or a data quality issue

ofc_ref_num_31.validation_status: PASS - These will dedupe correctly (same text + URL)

parser_behavior.extraction_method: Finds all occurrences of '(Ref: OFC #N)' pattern in HTML table cells
parser_behavior.deduplication: None - creates one entry per occurrence
parser_behavior.url_fallback: If URL missing, checks if same OFC ref_num was seen before and reuses URL
parser_behavior.text_extraction: Removes '(Ref: OFC #N)' pattern and trailing 'Source' label from cell text

validation_requirements.error_condition: If duplicates have different text/URLs -> ERROR
